name: 07_Image_Super_Resolution_SRCNN_Set5_DL
query: Hola, necesito crear un proyecto para super-resolución de imágenes usando el modelo SRCNN con el conjunto de datos Set5 (disponible en `https://huggingface.co/datasets/eugenesiow/Set5`). Carga el conjunto de datos en `src/data_loader.py`. Al cargar los datos, incluye pasos de preprocesamiento de imágenes como redimensionamiento y normalización, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. El modelo SRCNN debe ser cargado y utilizado en `src/model.py`. Guarda 5 conjuntos de imágenes de comparación, ampliando los detalles, como `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png`, y los resultados de super-resolución como `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png`. Las imágenes generadas deben ser de alta calidad y mostrar claramente las mejoras.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Set5" (disponible en "Hugging Face") se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.
El preprocesamiento de imágenes, incluyendo redimensionamiento y normalización, se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se guardan cinco conjuntos de imágenes de comparación, con detalles ampliados, y se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png`.
Los resultados de super-resolución se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png`.

preferences.criteria: El proyecto debe generar imágenes de super-resolución de alta calidad y claras, con comparaciones detalladas.
Se deben incluir imágenes de salida bien organizadas, destacando las mejoras clave.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Set5" (disponible en "Hugging Face") se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement actual loading of the "Set5" dataset from Hugging Face. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with a `load_dataset()` function that simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any real dataset loading logic. There are no imports of `datasets` (Hugging Face library), no calls to `datasets.load_dataset("Set5")` or any equivalent, and no actual data processing. The file explicitly labels itself as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: El preprocesamiento de imágenes, incluyendo redimensionamiento y normalización, se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement actual image preprocessing (resizing and normalization). It only contains a placeholder `build_transforms()` function that returns a static list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` — no real image resizing or normalization logic is implemented. The `load_dataset()` function is also a stub returning a hardcoded dictionary. There is no actual use of libraries like PIL, OpenCV, torchvision transforms, or any real preprocessing pipeline. The file is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" placeholder, not a functional implementation.

<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement the SRCNN model. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation that lists "SRCNN" as a keyword string in a list (`KEYWORDS = ["SRCNN"]`) but never actually defines, imports, or uses an SRCNN neural network architecture. There is no convolutional neural network definition, no PyTorch/TensorFlow model class, no layers (patch extraction, non-linear mapping, reconstruction), and no actual SRCNN implementation. The `build_model()` function simply returns a string `"model placeholder"`. The trajectory evidence also confirms no SRCNN model was executed, imported, or trained.

judge_stats.criteria: Se guardan cinco conjuntos de imágenes de comparación, con detalles ampliados, y se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png` exists in the workspace, but its content shows an error rather than a valid image. The file content reveals a `litellm.BadRequestError` related to an image format mismatch (the image was specified as `image/jpeg` but appears to be `image/png`), indicating the file was not successfully generated as a proper comparison image with five sets of enlarged details. The trajectory evidence also confirms no steps were recorded for creating this file with the required comparison sets.

judge_stats.criteria: Los resultados de super-resolución se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png` does exist in the workspace structure, but its content reveals an error rather than a valid image. The file contains an error message indicating a `litellm.BadRequestError` related to an image format mismatch (the image was specified as `image/jpeg` but appears to be `image/png`). This means the file was not successfully generated as a proper PNG image containing super-resolution results — it only holds an error string. Therefore, the requirement of saving valid super-resolution results to `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png` is not satisfied.
